title: Kubernetes Purpose, Architecture, Reconciliation, and Environment Evidence · Independent Platform Trial
summary: An independent delivery team receives an unfamiliar namespace, failure domain, and policy constraint and must transfer kubernetes purpose, architecture, reconciliation, and environment evidence into a new cluster architecture and reconciliation evidence map. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of cluster architecture and reconciliation evidence map with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, cau…
objectives: Trace API server, etcd, scheduler, controller manager, kubelet, container runtime, network proxy, DNS, and add-on responsibilities for one workload transition.
Explain how API requests, admission, persisted desired state, watches, caches, and component status relate without treating any one cache as authority.
Trace desired state, observed state, controller decisions, idempotent actions, status updates, and eventual convergence through a changed failure.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab K8s Control Plane Node Components
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab team sees kubectl 1.36.2, an unfamiliar current context, a healthy API endpoint, and a Deployment whose desired replicas differ from observed status. Trace client, API server, admission, storage, controller, scheduler, kubelet, and runtime authority without treating reconciliation as application correctness. Competency probe: Trace API server, etcd, scheduler, controller manager, kubelet, container runtime, network proxy, DNS, and add-on responsibilities for one workload transition.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab K8s Control Plane Node Components.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace api server, etcd, scheduler, controller manager, kubelet, container runtime, network proxy, dns, and add-on responsibilities for one workload transition. For Commit to the mission model · Lab K8s Control Plane Node Components, keep K8s Control Plane Node Components connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A lab team sees kubectl 1.36.2, an unfamiliar current context, a healthy API endpoint, and a Deployment whose desired replicas differ from observed status. Trace client, API server, admission, storage, controller, scheduler, kubelet, and runtime authority without treating reconciliation as application correctness. Competency probe: Trace API server, etcd, scheduler, controller manager, kubelet, container runtime, network proxy, DNS, and add-on responsibilities for one workload transition.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab K8s Version Context Baseline
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-version-context-baseline-1h84vp". Tailor its architecture-evidence.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab K8s Version Context Baseline.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Version Context Baseline into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab K8s Version Context Baseline, keep K8s Version Context Baseline connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-version-context-baseline-1h84vp
  - competency: k8s-version-context-baseline
    artifact: architecture-evidence.yaml
    decision: version context baseline
    environment: three-node dual-stack staging cluster across two zones
    fault-injection: restore a claim in a different topology and numeric identity
    retained-proof: EndpointSlice DNS policy route and packet-path correlation
    case-key: 1h84vp
    change: |
      request-path: kubectl -> api-server -> admission -> etcd
      controller-watch: desired-vs-observed
      node-path: scheduler -> kubelet -> runtime
      convergence: status-condition
    verify: kubectl version && kubectl cluster-info && kubectl get --raw /readyz?verbose
    failure: Reject client-version-only evidence, direct-etcd application access, or a claim that reconciliation guarantees application correctness.
    repair: Capture current context, server APIs, component authority, desired state, observed status, and the remaining distribution-specific boundary.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated K8s Version Context Baseline condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, copy one familiar manifest or kubectl command, omit context, API discovery, desired-versus-observed status, failure, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer checks, then accept an Applied response as production proof.
